description: In a climactic confrontation in a secluded area, May uses Hannah as bait to draw out Tobias. She faces Tobias and convinces him to let go of his guilt and fixation on Hannah, urging him to stop clinging to life and accept responsibility for his actions. Hannah confronts Tobias, and ultimately, May's compassion and words free Hannah as Tobias dissipates.

transcript.line: It's okay. It's okay. Tell him I won't hurt you.
Listen, we believe you're trapped between two worlds, this world...
And hell. I'm being dragged to hell for what I did.
Just loosen a few bolts, and then you'd visit. It was the best part of my day. I'm sorry. I... I didn't mean for anyone to get hurt.
Oh, no. Tobias. How could you?
I killed them. I know. And even worse, everyone started blaming you. I tr... I tried to stop them.
I was trying to protect you from them, trying to atone from my sin. Hannah, please, maybe if you forgive me, I won't go to hell.

And he won't. You can't undo what's been done. That will be with you forever. But trying to hold on to this life, clinging to the person you thought you could be, that's hell. And you're dragging her down with you. You have to let go before my people come and make you do it. If you care about her, and I know you do, let her go. Let the girl go, Tobias. Let the girl go.
